title: The 90-Day Payback Calculator: Sizing AI Builds From $5K Pilots to $250K Systems
subtitle: For ops leaders at 10-200 person service firms. Plug in seven numbers, get back the tier, payback window, and Year 1 ROI your CFO would actually approve.

inputs.label: Weekly hours consumed by the process
inputs.hint: Total team time on the target workflow each week. Include handoffs and rework.

inputs.label: Fully-loaded hourly cost
inputs.hint: Salary + benefits + overhead, divided by hours worked. If you don't have it, use 1.4x hourly salary.

inputs.label: Process volume
inputs.hint: How many times this process runs per month. Onboardings, intake forms, proposals, reports, invoices.

inputs.label: Rework rate
inputs.hint: Portion of transactions that get redone or kicked back. Most service firm processes sit between 8% and 25%.

inputs.label: Process complexity (1-5)
inputs.hint: 1 = single repetitive task. 3 = a few decision points with documented rules. 5 = many edge cases and multiple systems to reconcile.

inputs.label: Estimated build cost
inputs.hint: Use the tier guidance if you don't have a quote yet. Most mid-market builds land between $25K and $150K.

inputs.label: Monthly maintenance
inputs.hint: API calls, hosting, model costs, plus human review time. Most builds land between $300 and $2,500/mo.

outputs.label: Net Year 1 ROI
outputs.formula: ((hours_per_week * 52 * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (((hours_per_week * 52 * hourly_cost * 0.75) / Math.max(1 - (rework_rate / 100), 0.5)) * (rework_rate / 100)) - build_cost - (12 * monthly_maintenance)) / build_cost * 100

outputs.label: Payback period
outputs.formula: build_cost / Math.max(((hours_per_week * 52 * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (((hours_per_week * 52 * hourly_cost * 0.75) / Math.max(1 - (rework_rate / 100), 0.5)) * (rework_rate / 100))) / 12 - monthly_maintenance, 1)

recommendations.text: Run a $5K-$15K pilot. Single-process RPA-style automation: invoice processing, intake form parsing, or data entry between two systems. If this is your first AI build, start here, prove the math, then go bigger.
Sits in the $15K-$50K single-process band. The complexity is low enough that one workflow handles it. Watch the build cost: a complexity-2 process has no business eating $75K.

Mid-complexity with sub-9-month payback puts this in the $50K-$150K multi-agent band. Two or three agents passing structured handoffs, human review on 15-20% of cases. The bulk of mid-market builds live here.

High-complexity workflow earning a $150K-$250K enterprise build. Custom data integration, governance, audit trails. Don't start here unless you've already shipped a smaller build with a vendor you trust.

The math says don't build it yet. Payback past 18 months means the bottleneck is upstream of where AI helps. Clean the input data, document decision logic, narrow scope to a clear happy path, then come back to this calculator.
Volume is doing the work here. 200+ transactions/month with low complexity makes this a strong pilot candidate even with a borderline payback window. Unit economics get better the longer it runs.

Before signing a quote, run the three preconditions: structured input data, documentable decision rules, narrow initial scope covering 70%+ of cases. A process that fails any of these captures roughly 40% of projected ROI in Year 1, generously.

capture_cta.headline: Want the tier recommendation in writing?
capture_cta.description: Send me your top three processes with these seven inputs filled in. I'll return the tier, a rough scope of what each build looks like, and which one to ship first.
